records.ety: [OE. wernen, AS. weornan, wyrnan. Cf. Warn to admonish.]

[OE. warnen, warnien, AS. warnian, wearnian, to take heed, to warn; akin to AS. wearn denial, refusal, OS. warning, wernian, to refuse, OHG. warnen, G. warnen to warn, OFries. warna, werna, Icel. varna to refuse; and probably to E. wary. <?/<?/<?/<?/.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Warned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Warning.]

defs: To make ware or aware; to give previous information to; to give notice to; to notify; to admonish; hence, to notify or summon by authority; as, to warn a town meeting; to warn a tenant to quit a house.
quotes: Cornelius the centurion . . . was warned from God by an holy angel to send for thee.
Who is it that hath warned us to the walls?

defs: To give notice to, of approaching or probable danger or evil; to caution against anything that may prove injurious.
